text.title: caching-strategy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for caching-strategy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Cache stampede (thundering herd): N concurrent misses all hit the DB simultaneously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bare cache-aside with no stampede protection sends every concurrent waiter to the DB on miss. Under load, this can produce N times single-request DB load, exactly reproducing the original problem.

failure-mode.description: Using pickle to serialize cache values in Redis.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pickle deserializes arbitrary Python objects. If cache contents are attacker-controlled, deserialization is remote code execution. Use JSON or msgpack with schema validation.

failure-mode.description: Importing redis.Redis (sync) in async FastAPI code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sync Redis calls block the event loop, serializing all concurrent requests. Import redis.asyncio.Redis for async services.

failure-mode.description: Agent sets TTL=86400 by default without computing it from staleness tolerance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 24-hour TTL is too long for most user-visible data. Compute TTL from the agreed staleness tolerance (e.g., 1800s for user profile if 30min stale is acceptable). Stale data for 24h produces customer complaints.

failure-mode.description: Cache key includes mutable or high-cardinality arguments (timestamps, request IDs).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A key that encodes request_id or current timestamp generates a new key per request, producing 0% hit rate and unbounded key growth in Redis.

failure-mode.description: Hot key: a single Redis key receiving all traffic (e.g., global feature flag served to every request).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A single Redis key is bound to one shard. At extreme traffic, it saturates network bandwidth or CPU for that shard. Mitigate with local in-process cache with short TTL + Redis fallback.

failure-mode.description: Adding a cache layer without Prometheus metrics in the same PR.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cache effectiveness is invisible without hit-rate monitoring. An unmonitored cache may have 10% hit rate (wasted infrastructure) or cause subtle staleness bugs that are only visible via metrics divergence.
